Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand User Input The user provides a database schema PostgreSQL with three tables order_event_log orders and resources They provide sample data for all three tables They define an Assumed Normal Process Flow with 7 steps in a specific order They give a prompt instruction for a Target LLM to Identify anomalies undesirable behaviors in the event log Hypothesize causes Propose SQL queries to investigate these hypotheses The user is essentially asking me to act as the Target LLM and fulfill the prompt based on the provided data and schema

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Data and Schema against Normal Flow Normal Flow Register Order Perform Credit Check Validate Stock Confirm Shipment Ship Goods Issue Invoice Receive Payment

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 Register Order Perform Credit Check Validate Stock Confirm Shipment Ship Goods Issue Invoice Receive Payment Matches normal flow exactly No anomalies

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 Register Order Confirm Shipment Ship Goods Perform Credit Check Validate Stock Issue Invoice Receive Payment Anomalies Shipment confirmed and shipped before credit check and stock validation Confirm Shipment and Ship Goods happen way too early Credit check happens after shipping High risk Stock validation happens after shipping Impossible logically but data shows it

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Hypotheses System bypassed workflow rules priority order override without proper checks manual intervention error data entry timestamp errors

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 Register Order Perform Credit Check Ship Goods Issue Invoice Confirm Shipment Receive Payment Anomalies Missing Validate Stock entirely Ship Goods happens before Confirm Shipment and Issue Invoice Confirm Shipment happens after shipping and invoicing

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Hypotheses Process violation rushed order system glitch allowing out of order events manual override for expedited shipping missing data entry for stock validation

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 Register Order Receive Payment Issue Invoice Confirm Shipment Ship Goods Anomalies Receive Payment happens before Issue Invoice Missing credit check and stock validation shipment scheduled equals N but still shipped

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Hypotheses Pre payment order maybe COD or advance payment system skipped mandatory checks data quality issue policy violation for high value order 3000

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Identify Key Anomalies and Hypothesize Causes I will categorize them for clarity Out of Sequence Execution Cases 1002 1003 1004 deviate significantly from the flow Missing Mandatory Steps Case 1003 missing Validate Stock Case 1004 missing Perform Credit Check and Validate Stock Logical Policy Violations Shipping before credit stock checks 1002 1003 Payment before invoicing 1004 Shipment confirmed as N but still shipped 1004

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypotheses System Workflow Bypass ERP or order management system allows manual overrides or lacks hard constraints Policy Exceptions Priority orders 1002 might have expedited workflows but still risky Data Entry Timestamp Errors Events logged out of order due to batch processing or delayed system updates Training Compliance Issues Staff skipping steps to meet SLAs

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Formulate SQL Queries to Investigate I need to write SQL queries that target these anomalies without hardcoding specific case ids if possible or using them as examples The prompt says propose relevant SQL queries to investigate these hypotheses further

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Query 1 Detect Out of Sequence Events Find cases where Ship Goods or Confirm Shipment occurs before Perform Credit Check or Validate Stock

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets refine Query 1 Find cases where shipping occurred before credit check

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Query 2 Detect Missing Mandatory Steps Find cases missing Validate Stock or Perform Credit Check

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Query 3 Detect Payment Before Invoicing

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Query 4 Check for Contradictory Additional Info Shipment scheduled equals N but shipped

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Query 5 Correlate with Order Type and Value to see if priority or high value orders bypass checks

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: I should also include a query using LAG to generally detect out of order events based on the expected sequence but that is complex I will stick to targeted practical queries that directly test the hypotheses

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Structure the Response Acknowledge the prompt and act as the Target LLM Section 1 Identified Anomalies and Undesirable Behaviors Section 2 Hypothesized Causes Section 3 Proposed SQL Queries with explanations Ensure queries are PostgreSQL compatible and use the provided schema

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction Refinement during thought Check Query 2 COUNT CASE works but SUM casting is more PostgreSQL idiomatic I will stick to standard SQL or clear PG syntax Query 1 and 3 use self joins on the event log That is fine I should also provide a query that uses LAG or window functions to show a more advanced process mining approach Actually keeping it simple and targeted to the specific anomalies found is better

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Anomaly Check 1002 major sequence violation shipping before checks 1003 missing Validate Stock ship before Confirm confirm after Ship 1004 missing Credit and Stock payment before Invoice contradictory confirmation flag All match my analysis

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction Verification during drafting Check additional_info parsing LIKE shipment scheduled equals N works orders join LEFT JOIN is correct for missing steps PostgreSQL specific COUNT FILTER is very PostgreSQL I will use that for elegance I will make sure queries are ready to run

Name: Validation - PE
Text: I will ensure the SQL uses the exact column names and table names provided I will double check the additional_info column format It is VARCHAR with key value pairs LIKE works
